revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hirose-jinja (Sayama)]][[ja:広瀬神社 (狭山市)]] {{Expand Japanese|広瀬神社 (狭山市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hirose Shrine (Q11485625) | native_name = 広瀬神社 | image = Hirose jinja shrine.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hirose Shrine (Q11485625) | coordinates = {{coord|35.858613|139.38902}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Hirose Shrine'''|広瀬神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11546334|ltq=Q11546334|qid=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 21 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039739|ltq=Q135039739|qid=Q135039739|1=Nakahikahano Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Kitano Tenjinsha|lt=Kitano Tenjinsha|ja|北野天神社 (所沢市)|qid=Q11404276|1=Kitano Tenjinsha}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q824009|ltq=Q824009|qid=Q824009|1=Sayama}} * {{ill|Musashi Province|lt=Musashi Province|ar|محافظة موساشي|ca|Província de Musashi|ckb|پارێزگای موساشی|cs|Provincie Musaši|de|Provinz Musashi|en|Musashi Province|eo|Provinco Musaŝi|es|Provincia de Musashi|fa|ولایت موساشی|fr|Province de Musashi|gl|Provincia de Musashi|hak|Musashi-koet|id|Provinsi Musashi|it|Musashi (provincia)|ja|武蔵国|ko|무사시국|lt|Musašio žemė|nl|Musashi (provincie)|pl|Prowincja Musashi|pt|Província de Musashi|ru|Мусаси (провинция)|simple|Musashi Province|sk|Musaši (provincia)|sr|Провинција Мусаши|th|แคว้นมูซาชิ|tr|Musashi (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Мусаші|vi|Musashi (tỉnh)|zh_min_nan|Musasi-kok|zh_yue|武藏國|zh|武藏國|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} * {{ill|Q1153801|ltq=Q1153801|qid=Q1153801|1=Iruma District, Saitama}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.858613|139.38902}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Hirose jinja shrine.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181709 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q90139226|ltq=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == postal code (P281) == * 350-1319https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3030005003819 == Commons category (P373) == * Hirose-jinja (Sayama) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '埼玉県狭山市広瀬2-23-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '埼玉県狭山市上広瀬', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Hirose-jinja (Sayama)}} [[JA:広瀬神社 (狭山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11485625}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Hirose Shrine | image = [[ファイル:Hirose_jinja_shrine.jpg|270px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|qid=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sayama|Q824009|qid=Q824009|1=Sayama}} Hirose 2-23-1 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|51|30.8|N|139|23|20.6|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = Waka-Uka-no-Me-no-Mikoto and five associated deities | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} | founded = (trad.) reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} | architecture = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | annual_festival = {{ill|lt=April 4|Q2508|qid=Q2508|1=April 4}} }} '''Hirose Shrine''' (ひろせじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Hirose, {{ill|lt=Sayama|Q824009|qid=Q824009|1=Sayama}}, {{ill|lt=Saitama Prefecture|Q128186|qid=Q128186|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It is a shikinaisha and was formerly ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deity * Waka-Uka-no-Me-no-Mikoto ; Co-enshrined * Kami-Hono-Musubi-no-Mikoto * Konohana-Sakuya-Hime-no-Mikoto * Yachimata-Hiko-no-Mikoto * Yachimata-Hime-no-Mikoto * Kunado-no-Mikoto The first two correspond to deities of {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|qid=Q705281|1=Hirose Taisha}} in Yamato. Additional deities were incorporated at shrine mergers in 1907. === History === Shrine tradition says {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|qid=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} enshrined deities here during his eastern campaign, comparing the landscape to Kawai in Yamato (site of Hirose Taisha). Modern scholarship sees the shrine name "Hirose" as likely a local topographic name (wide shallows at river confluence), not necessarily proof of direct branch origin from Yamato. It is nonetheless an old influential shrine in Iruma District; in 850 it was listed as an official shrine in the ''Nihon Montoku Tenno Jitsuroku,'' and in 927 it appears in the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho.'' In the Edo period, Hozoji (now extinct) served as betto temple. In 1823, old records were lost in a temple fire. In 1873 it was made a gosha, then promoted to prefectural shrine in 1874 after local petition efforts by Shimizu Sotoku. At that time a school for training shrine clergy was also established in the precinct. In 1907, Atago and Sengen shrines were merged into it. In 1909, the honden was moved to the center of the grounds and a new haiden built. Current structures were repaired/rebuilt in 1975. === Precinct === * Subsidiary shrines: Inari Shrine, Hachiman Daijin Shrine, Kasumi Shrine (for local war dead) * Two giant zelkova trees (former count was three): about 800 years old, around 30 m high, over 6 m trunk girth; designated Saitama natural monument * Monument praising Shimizu Sotoku (erected 1914) There are no komainu in the precinct; a local tradition says the main deity is female and dislikes rough symbols. === Treasures === * Hanging Buddhist-style votive image donated by Hirose parishioners * Imperial calligraphy attributed to Emperor Suko * Inscribed swords (including one signed Bizen Osafune Sukesada) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Shimizu Sotoku|Q11560909|qid=Q11560909|1=Shimizu Sōtoku}} === References === * ''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten'' Vol. 11 * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Saitama'' * ''Dai Nihon Chimei Jisho'' Vol. 6 * ''Shinpen Musashi Fudokiko'' (Kami-Hirose village section) === External links === {{Commonscat|Hirose-jinja (Sayama)}} * Sayama City official pages on Hirose Shrine and its giant zelkova trees == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hirose Shrine (Q11485625)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hirose Shrine (Q11485625)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hirose Shrine (Q11485625)]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:狭山市の建築物]] [[Category:狭山市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Saitama Prefecture]]
